title: Pre-Market Briefing - 2026-06-25
markdown:
# Pre-Market Briefing

## 1. Market Tone
- **Risk-on tilt for semis/tech**: Nasdaq futures were noted up about 2% after **Micron**’s results, with chip stocks benefiting from tighter memory supply and improved Asia sentiment (The Motley Fool).
- **Broader tape mixed**: Wednesday’s close showed the **S&P 500** slightly down, **Nasdaq** down, and **Dow** up modestly, while **Bitcoin** was lower (The Motley Fool).
- **Banking tone supportive**: Large U.S. banks passed the Fed stress test, which should help sentiment for financials near the open (The Motley Fool).

## 2. Macro And Calendar
**Confirmed from source**
- **May core PCE** is expected to rise to **3.4% y/y** from **3.3%** in April; headline PCE expected at **4.1%**, the highest since April 2023 (The Motley Fool).
- Fed stress test results are already out and were positive for major banks (The Motley Fool).

**Unclear / not provided**
- No exact release time for PCE was included.
- No other calendar items were provided.

## 3. Earnings And Company News
- **Micron (** **MU** **)**: Shares were up around **17% premarket** after quarterly results showed exceptional demand, especially in data center business sales, which climbed more than sevenfold year over year (The Motley Fool).
  - Management said memory and storage shortages may take until **2028** to improve, and customers are signing **three- to five-year deals** for capacity (The Motley Fool).
  - Gross margin came in at **84.56%**, well above last year’s **39%** (The Motley Fool).
- **McCormick (** **MKC** **)**: Up around **2.5% premarket** after Q2 results with **net sales up 16.7%** and full-year EPS outlook reaffirmed (The Motley Fool).
- **U.S. Bancorp (** **USB** **)**: Confirmed a **3.8% quarterly dividend increase to $0.54/share** after passing the stress test (The Motley Fool).
- **JPMorgan (** **JPM** **)** and **Goldman Sachs (** **GS** **)** also passed the stress test, which is a positive backdrop for the banking group (The Motley Fool).

## 4. Top Movers And Catalysts
1. **MU** — biggest actionable mover in the email; earnings + supply shortage narrative + long-duration customer contracts.
2. **MKC** — modest premarket lift on earnings and reaffirmed guidance.
3. **USB / JPM / GS** — positive sector catalyst from stress test results and payout actions.
4. **SK Hynix** — rose 13% on potential U.S. listing momentum; relevant as a sector read-through, though not a U.S. trade setup in the source.

## 5. Ranked Buy Candidates
> Only one name looks strong enough from the provided material.

### 1) **MU** — primary swing candidate
- **Catalyst:** Earnings showed very strong data center demand, severe memory supply tightness, and exceptional gross margin expansion (The Motley Fool).
- **Why it might matter for a swing trade:** The combination of a major earnings beat narrative, a powerful AI/data-center read-through, and premarket gap momentum can sustain multi-day continuation if buyers defend the breakout.
- **What would invalidate the idea:** If the gap reverses hard, momentum fades, or the market treats the move as fully priced after the huge run-up in margin and demand expectations.
- **Must check before entry:** Intraday hold of the gap, volume confirmation, whether price bases above premarket highs, and whether broad semiconductor futures/major chip peers are supporting the move.
- **Buy logic:** Best viewed as a **catalyst-driven momentum swing**, but only if the opening action confirms strength rather than a fade.

### 2) **MKC** — lower-confidence follow-through candidate
- **Catalyst:** Q2 results showed sales growth and reaffirmed full-year EPS outlook (The Motley Fool).
- **Why it might matter for a swing trade:** A modest earnings reaction can work if the market starts rewarding stability and guidance confirmation.
- **What would invalidate the idea:** If the move is only a small premarket pop with weak continuation or if the open sells back into the prior range.
- **Must check before entry:** Whether the stock can hold above the opening range and whether volume expands enough to justify a follow-through trade.
- **Buy logic:** This is weaker than **MU**; only consider if price action confirms a clean post-earnings trend.

## 6. Swing Setup Board
| Ticker | Setup Type | Trigger | Stop / Invalidation | First Target Logic | Sizing Caution | Pass If |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **MU** | Catalyst Continuation / Breakout Momentum | Hold above premarket high and first 5–15 minute opening range on strong volume | Lose opening range / fail gap hold / sharp reversal through premarket support | Use the size of the gap and first intraday resistance; partial into first extension, trail if momentum persists | Gap move is already large; reduce size and avoid chasing extended candles | Open is weak, gap fades, or semis do not confirm |
| **MKC** | Trend Pullback / Reversal Bounce | Only if it holds early strength and reclaims opening range after a shallow pullback | Failure back below opening range or into premarket low | Prior day high / gap continuation if tape stays constructive | Smaller size; reaction is modest and may lack follow-through | If it opens flat-to-down or volume is thin |
| **USB** | Risk Watch | Not a primary entry from this email; watch for post-stress-test follow-through | If financials fail to respond or sector reverses after open | Limited; maybe prior resistance if sector strength broadens | Event is known; move may be already priced | No clean relative strength versus banks |
| **JPM** | Risk Watch | Same as above | Same as above | Same as above | Prefer watchlist only | No directional confirmation |
| **GS** | Risk Watch | Same as above | Same as above | Same as above | Prefer watchlist only | No directional confirmation |

## 7. Setup Watch
- **MU**: Highest priority. Watch for whether the premarket gap holds and whether semiconductor names confirm the move.
- **MKC**: Secondary watch only; needs clean post-open structure to be actionable.
- **Financials (** **USB**, **JPM**, **GS** **)**: Watch for sector strength after stress test headlines, but no clear swing setup is provided in the source.

## 8. Risk Watch
- **MU**: Elevated gap-risk after a ~17% premarket jump; a fast fade is possible if buyers fail to defend the open.
- **Sector crowding risk**: Chip strength is being driven by a very strong supply shortage narrative; if sentiment shifts, momentum can unwind quickly.
- **Bank news may be partially priced**: Stress test approval is positive, but not necessarily a fresh tradable catalyst once the open is underway.
- **Macro risk**: Upcoming PCE inflation expectations could influence rate-sensitive sectors if the print comes in hot, but timing/details were not included.

## 9. Sector And Theme Watch
- **Semiconductors / AI hardware**: Biggest theme in the email. **MU**’s results reinforced the memory-supply shortage story, and **SK Hynix** strength adds sector confirmation (The Motley Fool).
- **Data center demand**: Specifically called out as a major driver for **MU** (The Motley Fool).
- **Banks / financials**: Positive stress test results and dividend/buyback actions support the group (The Motley Fool).
- **Inflation / rates**: The market is looking ahead to PCE; hotter-than-expected inflation would matter for index and rate-sensitive trading (The Motley Fool).

## 10. Worth Reading
- “**Micron Pops on Data Center Demand**” — the most relevant item for active traders today (The Motley Fool).
- “**Wall Street Passes Fed Stress Test**” — useful for banking sector context (The Motley Fool).
- “**Next Up: Earnings and Inflation in Focus**” — gives the near-term macro backdrop (The Motley Fool).

## 11. Open Questions
- Will **MU** hold the premarket gap and build a durable opening range, or fade after the large reaction?
- Can **MKC** convert a modest earnings pop into a real trend day?
- Do banks turn the stress-test news into real follow-through, or does the move stall after the open?
- What time will the core PCE release hit, and will it alter risk appetite later in the session? The email did not specify timing.

## Notable Noise
- **SK Hynix**: Important as a sector read-through, but not a direct U.S. setup from the source.
- Motley Fool commentary on long-term investing and AI hype-cycle thoughts: interesting, but low immediate trading utility for the open.
